---
title: "5'_validation"
output: html_document
date: "2026-02-17"
---

```{r}
suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(data.table)
  library(ggplot2)
})

# ----------------------------
# User params
# ----------------------------
flank <- 500L                      # +/- flank around TSS
out_prefix <- "refseq_tss_overview" # output file prefix (csv + png)
keep_std_chr <- TRUE               # keep chr1-22, chrX, chrY by default

STD_CHR <- c(paste0("chr", 1:22), "chrX", "chrY")

# ---- Read refseq_all from disk ----
get_refseq_all <- function(rds = "data/refseq_all.rds", csv = "data/refseq_all.csv") {
  if (file.exists(rds)) {
    x <- readRDS(rds)
    return(x)
  }
  if (file.exists(csv)) {
    x <- data.table::fread(csv, data.table = FALSE)
    # enforce integer columns if present
    for (nm in intersect(c("txStart","txEnd","cdsStart","cdsEnd","exonCount"), names(x))) {
      x[[nm]] <- as.integer(x[[nm]])
    }
    return(x)
  }
  stop("refseq_all not found: ", rds, " or ", csv)
}

refseq_all <- get_refseq_all()

# ----------------------------
# 0) Normalize refseq_all columns + compute transcript-level TSS
# ----------------------------
rs <- as.data.table(refseq_all)

# normalize chr column name to "chr"
if (!("chr" %in% names(rs))) {
  chr_col <- names(rs)[tolower(names(rs)) %in% c("chrom", "chr")][1]
  if (is.na(chr_col)) stop("refseq_all: cannot find chrom/chr column")
  setnames(rs, chr_col, "chr")
}

need <- c("chr", "txStart", "txEnd", "strand", "name2")
if (!all(need %in% names(rs))) {
  stop("refseq_all missing: ", paste(setdiff(need, names(rs)), collapse = ", "))
}

# basic cleaning
rs <- rs[
  !is.na(name2) & name2 != "" &
    !is.na(chr) & !is.na(txStart) & !is.na(txEnd) &
    !is.na(strand) & strand %in% c("+", "-") &
    txEnd >= txStart
]

if (keep_std_chr) {
  rs <- rs[chr %chin% STD_CHR]
}

# TSS (1-based): + strand: txStart + 1; - strand: txEnd
. <- rs[, tss := fifelse(strand == "-", as.integer(txEnd), as.integer(txStart) + 1L)]
. <- rs[, tss := as.integer(tss)]

# transcript-level +/- flank window
# Assigning to a null/dummy variable prevents the table from printing
. <- rs[, `:=`(
  win_start = pmax(1L, tss - flank),
  win_end   = tss + flank
)]

cat("Rows (transcripts) after cleaning:", nrow(rs), "\n")
cat("Unique genes:", uniqueN(rs$name2), "\n\n")


# ----------------------------
# 1) Distribution: transcripts per gene
# ----------------------------
tx_per_gene <- rs[, .N, by = .(gene = name2)][order(-N)]
setnames(tx_per_gene, "N", "n_tx")

# Summary stats
tx_stats <- tx_per_gene[, .(
  n_genes = .N,
  mean_tx = mean(n_tx),
  median_tx = median(n_tx),
  p05 = quantile(n_tx, 0.05),
  p95 = quantile(n_tx, 0.95),
  max_tx = max(n_tx)
)]
print(tx_stats)

# --- How many genes have 1 transcript vs >1? ---
tx_counts <- tx_per_gene[, .(
  n_genes_total = .N,
  n_genes_1tx   = sum(n_tx == 1),
  n_genes_gt1tx = sum(n_tx > 1),
  frac_1tx      = sum(n_tx == 1) / .N,
  frac_gt1tx    = sum(n_tx > 1) / .N
)]
print(tx_counts)


tss_per_gene <- rs[, .(n_unique_tss = uniqueN(tss)), by = .(gene = name2)]
tss_counts2 <- tss_per_gene[, .(
  n_genes_total = .N,
  n_genes_1tss   = sum(n_unique_tss == 1),
  n_genes_gt1tss = sum(n_unique_tss > 1),
  frac_1tss      = sum(n_unique_tss == 1) / .N,
  frac_gt1tss    = sum(n_unique_tss > 1) / .N
)]
print(tss_counts2)
```


```{r}
# ----------------------------
# 2) Within-gene TSS dispersion
# ----------------------------

# (a) per-gene range
tss_range <- rs[, .(
  n_tx = .N,
  tss_min = min(tss),
  tss_max = max(tss),
  tss_range_bp = max(tss) - min(tss)
), by = .(gene = name2)][order(-tss_range_bp)]

range_stats <- tss_range[, .(
  n_genes = .N,
  mean_range = mean(tss_range_bp),
  median_range = median(tss_range_bp),
  p05 = quantile(tss_range_bp, 0.05),
  p95 = quantile(tss_range_bp, 0.95),
  max_range = max(tss_range_bp)
)]
print(range_stats)

# (b) adjacent diffs
adj_diffs <- rs[order(name2, chr, tss)][
  , .(adj_diff_bp = diff(unique(tss))), by = .(gene = name2)
][!is.na(adj_diff_bp)]

adj_stats <- adj_diffs[, .(
  n_diffs = .N,
  mean_adj = mean(adj_diff_bp),
  median_adj = median(adj_diff_bp),
  p05 = quantile(adj_diff_bp, 0.05),
  p95 = quantile(adj_diff_bp, 0.95),
  max_adj = max(adj_diff_bp)
)]
print(adj_stats)

# Then adj_stats summarizes the distribution of those adjacent gaps across all genes (pooled together).
# 
# n_diffs = 30302 = total number of adjacent gaps you observed across all genes
# median_adj = 1096 bp = typical spacing between alternative TSS when a gene has >1 unique TSS
# p05 = 7 bp = 5% of adjacent gaps are extremely close (near-duplicate TSS calls)
# p95 = 71872 bp = 95% of adjacent gaps are < ~72 kb
```


```{r}
suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(data.table)
})

# ----------------------------
# 3) 5′-most TSS vs others (multi-TSS genes only)
# ----------------------------

# Assumes rs already exists and has at least:
# chr, strand, txStart, txEnd, name2 (gene symbol), and tss already computed.
# Also assumes win_start / win_end exist OR we will compute them here.

flank <- 500L

rs <- as.data.table(rs)

# make sure strand is character (your dummy assignment style)
. <- rs[, strand := as.character(strand)]

# standardize gene column name for downstream joins
if (!("gene" %in% names(rs))) {
  if ("name2" %in% names(rs)) {
    . <- setnames(rs, "name2", "gene")
  } else {
    stop("rs must contain either 'gene' or 'name2' column.")
  }
}

# ensure TSS + windows exist (safe even if already present)
if (!("tss" %in% names(rs))) stop("rs is missing 'tss' column. Compute tss first.")
if (!all(c("win_start", "win_end") %in% names(rs))) {
  . <- rs[, `:=`(
    win_start = pmax(1L, as.integer(tss) - flank),
    win_end   = as.integer(tss) + flank
  )]
}

# ---- Focus ONLY on genes with >= 2 UNIQUE TSS ----
tss_per_gene <- rs[, .(n_unique_tss = uniqueN(tss)), by = .(gene)]
multi_genes  <- tss_per_gene[n_unique_tss >= 2, gene]

rs_multi <- rs[gene %chin% multi_genes]

# (Optional but recommended)
# If a gene appears on multiple chromosomes/strands in annotation, overlap concept becomes weird.
# Keep only (gene, chr, strand) groups with >=2 unique TSS.
grp_ok <- rs_multi[, .(n_unique_tss = uniqueN(tss)), by = .(gene, chr, strand)][n_unique_tss >= 2]
rs_multi <- rs_multi[grp_ok, on = .(gene, chr, strand)]

# ---- Pick 5′-most transcript per (gene, chr, strand) ----
# 5′-most definition:
#   + strand: smallest genomic coordinate TSS
#   - strand: largest genomic coordinate TSS
rs_5p <- rs_multi[
  , .SD[
    if (strand[1] == "-") which.max(tss) else which.min(tss)
  ][1],
  by = .(gene, chr, strand)
]

. <- rs_5p[, is_5p := TRUE]

# ---- Join 5′ info back to all transcripts in multi set ----
# Now both sides have gene/chr/strand so merge is clean.
rs2 <- merge(
  rs_multi,
  rs_5p[, .(gene, chr, strand,
            tss_5p = tss,
            win_start_5p = win_start,
            win_end_5p   = win_end)],
  by = c("gene", "chr", "strand"),
  all.x = TRUE
)

# mark whether each row is the chosen 5′ transcript (by exact tss match)
. <- rs2[, is_5p := (!is.na(tss_5p) & tss == tss_5p)]

# keep only "other" transcripts (non-5′) for overlap comparison
rs_other <- rs2[is_5p == FALSE]

# ---- Compute overlap of each non-5′ window with the 5′ window ----
# overlap length between [win_start, win_end] and [win_start_5p, win_end_5p]
# both inclusive; window length = 2*flank + 1
win_len <- 2L * flank + 1L

rs_other[, `:=`(
  ov_start = pmax(win_start,  win_start_5p),
  ov_end   = pmin(win_end,    win_end_5p)
)]

rs_other[, ov_bp := pmax(0L, as.integer(ov_end - ov_start + 1L))]
rs_other[, ov_frac := ov_bp / win_len]

# ---- Summaries you’ll likely want ----

# (A) How many genes/groups are we analyzing now?
multi_summary <- list(
  genes_total_in_rs         = uniqueN(rs$gene),
  genes_with_multi_unique_tss = uniqueN(multi_genes),
  gene_chr_strand_groups_multi = uniqueN(rs_multi[, paste(gene, chr, strand, sep="|")]),
  comparisons_n_non5p_rows  = nrow(rs_other)
)

multi_summary

# (B) per (gene, chr, strand): distribution of overlap fractions across "other" TSS
overlap_by_group <- rs_other[, .(
  n_other_tss = .N,
  mean_ov_frac = mean(ov_frac, na.rm = TRUE),
  median_ov_frac = median(ov_frac, na.rm = TRUE),
  p05_ov_frac = as.numeric(quantile(ov_frac, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE)),
  p95_ov_frac = as.numeric(quantile(ov_frac, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE))
), by = .(gene, chr, strand)]

# (C) overall overlap fraction distribution across all non-5′ transcripts
overall_ov_stats <- rs_other[, .(
  n = .N,
  mean = mean(ov_frac),
  median = median(ov_frac),
  p05 = as.numeric(quantile(ov_frac, 0.05)),
  p95 = as.numeric(quantile(ov_frac, 0.95))
)]

overall_ov_stats

# rs_other is the long table you can histogram (ov_frac) etc.


```

```{r}
suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(data.table)
  library(ggplot2)
})

# ---- 1) Overall quantiles / summary for overlap fraction ----
ov_summary <- rs_other[, .(
  n = .N,
  mean = mean(ov_frac, na.rm = TRUE),
  median = median(ov_frac, na.rm = TRUE),
  p05 = as.numeric(quantile(ov_frac, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE)),
  p25 = as.numeric(quantile(ov_frac, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE)),
  p75 = as.numeric(quantile(ov_frac, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE)),
  p95 = as.numeric(quantile(ov_frac, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE))
)]
ov_summary

# (optional) also summarize overlap in bp
ov_bp_summary <- rs_other[, .(
  n = .N,
  mean_bp = mean(ov_bp, na.rm = TRUE),
  median_bp = median(ov_bp, na.rm = TRUE),
  p05_bp = as.numeric(quantile(ov_bp, 0.05, na.rm = TRUE)),
  p25 = as.numeric(quantile(ov_bp, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE)),
  p75 = as.numeric(quantile(ov_bp, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE)),
  p95_bp = as.numeric(quantile(ov_bp, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE))
)]
ov_bp_summary


# ---- 2) Histogram: overlap fraction between 5′ window and other windows ----
# Because a lot of mass is at 0, a histogram is fine; optionally add a zoomed version too.

p_hist_ovfrac <- ggplot(rs_other, aes(x = ov_frac)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 60, color = "white") +
  theme_bw(base_size = 12) +
  labs(
    title = sprintf("Overlap between 5′-most TSS window and other TSS windows (±%d bp)", flank),
    subtitle = "Each point is a non-5′ transcript within multi-TSS gene groups (gene×chr×strand)",
    x = "Overlap fraction (intersection length / window length)",
    y = "# transcript comparisons"
  )
print(p_hist_ovfrac)

# ---- 3) (Optional) Zoom in on the non-zero overlaps only ----
p_hist_nonzero <- ggplot(rs_other[ov_frac > 0], aes(x = ov_frac)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 60, color = "white") +
  theme_bw(base_size = 12) +
  labs(
    title = sprintf("Non-zero overlaps only (±%d bp windows)", flank),
    subtitle = "Drops the large spike at 0 to show the distribution when windows intersect",
    x = "Overlap fraction",
    y = "# transcript comparisons"
  )
print(p_hist_nonzero)

# ---- 4) (Optional) Show CDF-like view via ECDF ----
p_ecdf <- ggplot(rs_other, aes(x = ov_frac)) +
  stat_ecdf() +
  theme_bw(base_size = 12) +
  labs(
    title = "ECDF of overlap fraction",
    subtitle = "Useful to read off fractions below/above thresholds (e.g., overlap=0)",
    x = "Overlap fraction",
    y = "ECDF"
  )
print(p_ecdf)

```
```{r}
library(data.table)

# rs must have: chr (or chrom), strand, txStart, txEnd, name2
# If rs already has tss, you can skip this block.
if (!("tss" %in% names(rs))) {
  rs <- as.data.table(rs)
  if (!("chr" %in% names(rs))) {
    chr_col <- names(rs)[tolower(names(rs)) %in% c("chrom", "chr")][1]
    setnames(rs, chr_col, "chr")
  }
  rs[, strand := as.character(strand)]
  rs[, tss := fifelse(strand == "-", as.integer(txEnd), as.integer(txStart) + 1L)]
}
```
```{r}
library(data.table)

build_multiTSS_windows <- function(rs, genes_multi, flank = 500L) {
  rs <- as.data.table(rs)

  # keep only multi-TSS genes
  dt <- rs[!is.na(gene) & gene %chin% genes_multi &
             !is.na(chr) & !is.na(strand) & !is.na(tss)]

  # unique TSS per gene×chr×strand (important!)
  dt_u <- unique(dt[, .(
    gene   = as.character(gene),
    chr    = as.character(chr),
    strand = as.character(strand),
    tss    = as.integer(tss)
  )])

  # compute 5′ TSS per gene×chr×strand
  # + strand: 5′ = smallest tss; - strand: 5′ = largest tss
  dt_u[, tss_5p := if (strand[1] == "-") max(tss) else min(tss),
       by = .(gene, chr, strand)]

  # rank from 5′ within gene×chr×strand
  dt_u[
    order(gene, chr, strand, ifelse(strand == "-", -tss, tss)),
    rank_from_5p := seq_len(.N),
    by = .(gene, chr, strand)
  ]

  dt_u[, dist_from_5p_bp := abs(tss - tss_5p)]

  # build TSS±flank window (1-based inclusive)
  dt_u[, `:=`(
    start = pmax(1L, tss - flank),
    end   = tss + flank
  )]

  # unique interval name encodes gene + chr + strand + tss
  dt_u[, name := sprintf("TSSVAR_%s_%s_%s_%d", gene, chr, strand, tss)]

  # final table
  dt_u[, .(chr, start, end, name, gene, strand, tss, tss_5p, dist_from_5p_bp, rank_from_5p)]
}

# build windows
# if you want to keep the name you were using:
genes_with_multi_unique_tss <- multi_genes

# now call with the correct object

flank <- 500L
tssvar_iv <- build_multiTSS_windows(rs, genes_with_multi_unique_tss, flank = flank)
setkey(tssvar_iv, chr, start, end)

tssvar_iv[, .N]
tssvar_iv[, uniqueN(gene)]
```

```{r}
library(data.table)

tss_lookup <- as.data.table(tssvar_iv)[
  , .(name, gene, dist_from_5p_bp, rank_from_5p)
]
setkey(tss_lookup, name)

```

```{r}
library(data.table)


pwd_for_pair_gene_agg_stream <- function(bedA, bedB, intervals_dt, lookup_dt,
                                         min_cov = 8L, min_cpgs = 1L,
                                         interval_chunk = 5000L,
                                         pair_label = "PAIR",
                                         sampleA = "A", sampleB = "B") {
  stopifnot(all(c("chr","start","end","name") %in% names(intervals_dt)))
  stopifnot("name" %in% names(lookup_dt))

  # ensure lookup has what we need
  need_lookup <- c("name","gene","dist_from_5p_bp","rank_from_5p")
  miss_lookup <- setdiff(need_lookup, names(lookup_dt))
  if (length(miss_lookup)) {
    stop("lookup_dt missing columns: ", paste(miss_lookup, collapse = ", "))
  }

  # copy + key lookup for fast merge
  lookup <- data.table::as.data.table(lookup_dt)[, ..need_lookup]
  data.table::setkey(lookup, name)

  iv <- data.table::as.data.table(intervals_dt)[, .(chr, start, end, name)]
  iv[, `:=`(chr = as.character(chr),
            start = as.integer(start),
            end   = as.integer(end),
            name  = as.character(name))]

  # chunk indices
  n <- nrow(iv)
  idx_chunks <- split(seq_len(n), ceiling(seq_len(n) / interval_chunk))

  # accumulator (small!)
  acc <- data.table(
    pair = character(),
    sampleA = character(),
    sampleB = character(),
    gene = character(),
    dist_from_5p_bp = integer(),
    rank_from_5p = integer(),
    sum_w = numeric(),        # sum(pwd_mean * n_cpg)
    sum_cpg = numeric(),      # sum(n_cpg)
    n_intervals = integer()
  )
  data.table::setkey(acc, pair, gene, dist_from_5p_bp, rank_from_5p)

  for (ii in seq_along(idx_chunks)) {
    idx <- idx_chunks[[ii]]
    iv_chunk <- iv[idx]

    # core computation: returns per-interval name + pwd_mean + n_cpg
    tbl <- pwd_for_pair_chunked(
      bedA, bedB, iv_chunk,
      min_cov = min_cov,
      min_cpgs = min_cpgs,
      interval_chunk = interval_chunk
    )
    if (!nrow(tbl)) { gc(); next }

    tbl <- data.table::as.data.table(tbl)[, .(name, pwd_mean, n_cpg)]
    tbl[, name := as.character(name)]
    data.table::setkey(tbl, name)

    # IMPORTANT FIX: inner join via merge (safe + deterministic)
    # keep only names that exist in lookup
    tbl2 <- merge(tbl, lookup, by = "name", all = FALSE)
    if (!nrow(tbl2)) { rm(tbl); gc(); next }

    # chunk aggregation to small table
    chunk_agg <- tbl2[, .(
      sum_w       = sum(pwd_mean * pmax(n_cpg, 1L), na.rm = TRUE),
      sum_cpg     = sum(pmax(n_cpg, 1L), na.rm = TRUE),
      n_intervals = .N
    ), by = .(gene, dist_from_5p_bp, rank_from_5p)]

    chunk_agg[, `:=`(pair = pair_label, sampleA = sampleA, sampleB = sampleB)]
    data.table::setkey(chunk_agg, pair, gene, dist_from_5p_bp, rank_from_5p)

    # add into accumulator
    acc <- merge(
      acc, chunk_agg,
      by = c("pair","gene","dist_from_5p_bp","rank_from_5p"),
      all = TRUE,
      suffixes = c("", ".i")
    )

    # coalesce + sum
    acc[, `:=`(
      sum_w       = fifelse(is.na(sum_w), 0, sum_w) + fifelse(is.na(sum_w.i), 0, sum_w.i),
      sum_cpg     = fifelse(is.na(sum_cpg), 0, sum_cpg) + fifelse(is.na(sum_cpg.i), 0, sum_cpg.i),
      n_intervals = fifelse(is.na(n_intervals), 0L, n_intervals) + fifelse(is.na(n_intervals.i), 0L, n_intervals.i),
      sampleA     = fifelse(is.na(sampleA), sampleA.i, sampleA),
      sampleB     = fifelse(is.na(sampleB), sampleB.i, sampleB)
    )]

    # drop merge temp cols
    acc[, c("sum_w.i","sum_cpg.i","n_intervals.i","sampleA.i","sampleB.i") := NULL]

    rm(tbl, tbl2, chunk_agg, iv_chunk)
    gc()
  }

  # finalize CpG-weighted mean PWD
  acc[, wmean_pwd := fifelse(sum_cpg > 0, sum_w / sum_cpg, NA_real_)]
  acc[]
}
STD_CHR <- c(paste0("chr", 1:22), "chrX", "chrY")

read_cpg_bed_dt <- function(path, min_cov = 8L) {
  dt <- fread(path, sep = "\t", header = FALSE,
              col.names = c("chr","start","end","beta","cov","meth","unmeth"),
              showProgress = FALSE)
  dt <- dt[chr %chin% STD_CHR]
  dt <- dt[!is.na(beta) & !is.na(cov) & cov >= min_cov,
           .(chr = as.character(chr),
             start = as.integer(start),      # 0-based CpG start
             beta = as.numeric(beta))]
  dt[, pos := start + 1L]  # convert to 1-based coordinate for overlap
  dt
}

pwd_for_pair_chunked <- function(pathA, pathB, intervals_dt,
                                 min_cov = 8L, min_cpgs = 1L,
                                 interval_chunk = 5000L) {

  A <- read_cpg_bed_dt(pathA, min_cov)
  B <- read_cpg_bed_dt(pathB, min_cov)

  # CpG-level inner join (shared CpGs)
  setkey(A, chr, start)
  setkey(B, chr, start)
  AB <- B[A, nomatch = 0L]     # i.* from B, plain cols from A

  if (!nrow(AB)) {
    return(data.table(name=character(), gene=character(),
                      pwd_mean=numeric(), n_cpg=integer()))
  }

  AB[, pwd := abs(beta - i.beta)]

  # points for foverlaps: start=end=pos
  AB_pts <- AB[, .(chr,
                   start = as.integer(pos),
                   end   = as.integer(pos),
                   pwd)]
  setkey(AB_pts, chr, start, end)

  # ensure intervals are keyed correctly
  iv <- as.data.table(intervals_dt)
  stopifnot(all(c("chr","start","end","name") %in% names(iv)))
  if (!("gene" %in% names(iv))) iv[, gene := NA_character_]
  iv[, `:=`(chr = as.character(chr),
            start = as.integer(start),
            end   = as.integer(end))]
  setkey(iv, chr, start, end)

  chrs <- intersect(unique(AB_pts$chr), unique(iv$chr))
  out_chr <- vector("list", length(chrs))

  for (ci in seq_along(chrs)) {
    ch <- chrs[ci]
    cpg_ch <- AB_pts[chr == ch]
    iv_ch  <- iv[chr == ch]
    if (!nrow(cpg_ch) || !nrow(iv_ch)) next

    # chunk intervals
    idx <- split(seq_len(nrow(iv_ch)),
                 ceiling(seq_len(nrow(iv_ch)) / interval_chunk))

    out_chunks <- vector("list", length(idx))
    for (k in seq_along(idx)) {
      iv_chunk <- iv_ch[idx[[k]], .(chr, start, end, name, gene)]
      setkey(iv_chunk, chr, start, end)

      j <- foverlaps(cpg_ch, iv_chunk, nomatch = 0L)
      if (!nrow(j)) next

      out_chunks[[k]] <- j[, .(
        pwd_mean = mean(pwd, na.rm = TRUE),
        n_cpg    = .N
      ), by = .(name, gene)]
    }

    out_chr[[ci]] <- rbindlist(out_chunks, use.names = TRUE, fill = TRUE)
    gc()
  }

  res <- rbindlist(out_chr, use.names = TRUE, fill = TRUE)
  if (!nrow(res)) return(res)

  res <- res[n_cpg >= min_cpgs]
  unique(res)
}

# ---- BED file paths (WGBS CpG BEDs) ----
bed_dir <- "data"

bed_paths <- c(
  EN = file.path(bed_dir, "EN.bed"),
  IN = file.path(bed_dir, "IN.bed"),
  JN = file.path(bed_dir, "JN.bed")
)

min_cov  <- 5L   # or 8L, whatever you want
min_cpgs <- 1L
```

```{r}

# Normal-only pairs
pairs_normal <- pairs[names(pairs) %chin% c("Normal EN–IN", "Normal EN–JN", "Normal IN–JN")]

# Use only needed columns for intervals
tssvar_iv_small <- as.data.table(tssvar_iv)[, .(chr, start, end, name)]
setkey(tssvar_iv_small, chr, start, end)

pwd_tssvar_gene_by_pair <- rbindlist(lapply(names(pairs_normal), function(pnm) {
  s1 <- pairs_normal[[pnm]][1]
  s2 <- pairs_normal[[pnm]][2]
  message("Running pair: ", pnm, " (", s1, " vs ", s2, ")")

  pwd_for_pair_gene_agg_stream(
    bedA = bed_paths[[s1]],
    bedB = bed_paths[[s2]],
    intervals_dt = tssvar_iv_small,
    lookup_dt = tss_lookup,
    min_cov = min_cov,
    min_cpgs = min_cpgs,
    interval_chunk = 1000L,      # you can tune this
    pair_label = pnm,
    sampleA = s1, sampleB = s2
  )
}), use.names = TRUE, fill = TRUE)
```
```{r}
# Average across the 3 normal pairs (simple mean across pairs)
pwd_tssvar_gene_avg <- pwd_tssvar_gene_by_pair[
  , .(
    mean_pwd_3pairs = mean(wmean_pwd, na.rm = TRUE),
    median_pwd_3pairs = median(wmean_pwd, na.rm = TRUE),
    n_pairs = uniqueN(pair),
    total_cpg = sum(sum_cpg, na.rm = TRUE),
    total_intervals = sum(n_intervals, na.rm = TRUE)
  ),
  by = .(gene, dist_from_5p_bp, rank_from_5p)
]
```



```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(data.table)
# Ensure mgcv is installed
if (!requireNamespace("mgcv", quietly = TRUE)) install.packages("mgcv")

# (A) Revised Distance Plot with GAM
p_dist <- ggplot(dt_plot, aes(x = dist_from_5p_bp, y = mean_pwd_3pairs)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.15, size = 0.6) +
  geom_smooth(method = "gam", formula = y ~ s(x, bs = "cs"), se = TRUE) + # Much faster for large n
  scale_x_continuous(trans = "log1p") +
  theme_bw(base_size = 12)

dt_plot <- as.data.table(pwd_tssvar_gene_avg)

# (A) PWD vs distance (bp) from 5′ most TSS
p_dist <- ggplot(dt_plot, aes(x = dist_from_5p_bp, y = mean_pwd_3pairs)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.15, size = 0.6) +
  geom_smooth(method = "loess", se = FALSE) + # se = FALSE fixes the memory error
  scale_x_continuous(trans = "log1p") +
  theme_bw(base_size = 12) +
  labs(title = "Multi-TSS genes: methylation divergence vs distance", 
       x = "Distance (log1p)", y = "Mean PWD")

p_dist2 <- ggplot(dt_plot, aes(x = dist_from_5p_bp, y = mean_pwd_3pairs)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.15, size = 0.6) +
  geom_smooth(method = "gam", formula = y ~ s(x, bs = "cs"), se = TRUE) + # Much faster for large n
  scale_x_continuous(trans = "log1p") +
  theme_bw(base_size = 12)

print(p_dist)
print(p_dist2)

# (B) PWD vs rank from 5′ (1 = 5′ most)
p_rank <- ggplot(dt_plot, aes(x = rank_from_5p, y = mean_pwd_3pairs)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.15, size = 0.6) +
  geom_smooth(method = "loess", se = TRUE, color = "red",size=1) +
  geom_quantile(quantiles = 0.5, color = "blue", size = 1) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 12) +
  labs(
    title = "Multi-TSS genes: methylation divergence vs TSS rank from 5′",
    subtitle = "Rank computed within gene×chr×strand; PWD averaged across 3 Normal pairs",
    x = "TSS rank from 5′ (1 = 5′ most)",
    y = "Mean PWD across 3 Normal pairs (CpG-weighted within window)"
  )
print(p_rank)
```
```{r}
library(data.table)

# tssvar_iv has one row per unique TSS window (gene×chr×strand×tss)
# If you want per gene overall (across chr/strand), do:
tss_per_gene <- tssvar_iv[, .(n_unique_tss = uniqueN(tss)), by = gene]

# distribution
dist_tbl <- tss_per_gene[, .N, by = n_unique_tss][order(n_unique_tss)]
dist_tbl

# quick summary stats
tss_per_gene_summary <- tss_per_gene[, .(
  n_genes = .N,
  mean_tss = mean(n_unique_tss),
  median_tss = median(n_unique_tss),
  p90 = as.numeric(quantile(n_unique_tss, 0.90)),
  p95 = as.numeric(quantile(n_unique_tss, 0.95)),
  max_tss = max(n_unique_tss)
)]
tss_per_gene_summary
```
```{r}
tss_per_group <- tssvar_iv[, .(n_unique_tss = uniqueN(tss)), by = .(gene, chr, strand)]
dist_group_tbl <- tss_per_group[, .N, by = n_unique_tss][order(n_unique_tss)]
dist_group_tbl
```

```{r}
library(data.table)
library(ggplot2)

dt_plot <- as.data.table(pwd_tssvar_gene_avg)

# guard against duplicated quantile breakpoints (happens if many dist=0)
qbreaks <- unique(as.numeric(quantile(dt_plot$dist_from_5p_bp,
                                     probs = seq(0, 1, 0.1),
                                     na.rm = TRUE)))
# if breaks collapse too much, fall back to fixed bins
if (length(qbreaks) < 3) {
  qbreaks <- c(-Inf, 0, 10, 100, 1e3, 1e4, 1e5, Inf)
}

dt_plot[, dist_bin := cut(dist_from_5p_bp, breaks = qbreaks, include.lowest = TRUE)]

bin_sum <- dt_plot[, .(
  n         = .N,
  mean_pwd  = mean(mean_pwd_3pairs, na.rm = TRUE),
  median_pwd= median(mean_pwd_3pairs, na.rm = TRUE)
), by = dist_bin][order(dist_bin)]

ggplot(bin_sum, aes(x = dist_bin, y = mean_pwd, group = 1)) +
  geom_line() +
  geom_point() +
  theme_bw(base_size = 12) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)) +
  labs(
    title = "Binned trend: mean PWD vs distance-from-5′",
    subtitle = "Bins are distance quantiles (fallback to fixed bins if needed)",
    x = "Distance-from-5′ bins",
    y = "Mean PWD across 3 Normal pairs"
  )
```
```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(data.table)

# 1. Create a grouping column for 5' status
dt_density <- as.data.table(pwd_tssvar_gene_avg)
dt_density[, tss_group := fifelse(rank_from_5p == 1, "5' Most (Rank 1)", "Non-5' Most (Rank > 1)")]

# 2. Generate the density plot
p_density <- ggplot(dt_density, aes(x = mean_pwd_3pairs, fill = tss_group)) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.4) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 12) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("5' Most (Rank 1)" = "#2c7bb6", 
                               "Non-5' Most (Rank > 1)" = "#d7191c")) +
  labs(
    title = "Distribution of Methylation Divergence (PWD)",
    subtitle = "Comparing 5'-most TSS vs. downstream TSS across 3 Normal pairs",
    x = "Mean PWD (CpG-weighted)",
    y = "Density",
    fill = "TSS Position"
  ) +
  # Optional: Zoom in on the x-axis if there are long tails
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, quantile(dt_density$mean_pwd_3pairs, 0.99, na.rm = TRUE)))

print(p_density)
```
```{r}
library(data.table)

# 1. Identify the TSS with the minimum PWD for each gene
# We only consider genes that actually have more than 1 TSS
gene_min_pwd <- dt_plot[, .(
    min_pwd_rank = rank_from_5p[which.min(mean_pwd_3pairs)],
    n_tss = .N
), by = gene][n_tss > 1]

# 2. Calculate the fraction where Rank 1 is NOT the minimum
is_not_lowest_count <- sum(gene_min_pwd$min_pwd_rank != 1)
total_multi_tss_genes <- nrow(gene_min_pwd)
fraction_not_lowest <- is_not_lowest_count / total_multi_tss_genes

# 3. Print the results
cat(sprintf("Total multi-TSS genes: %d\n", total_multi_tss_genes))
cat(sprintf("Genes where 5' (Rank 1) is NOT the lowest PWD: %d\n", is_not_lowest_count))
cat(sprintf("Fraction: %.2f%%\n", fraction_not_lowest * 100))

# Optional: View the distribution of which ranks actually ARE the lowest
table(gene_min_pwd$min_pwd_rank)
```
```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(data.table)

# Calculate Delta: (PWD of 5'-most TSS) - (Min PWD found in that gene)
# Positive values indicate the 5'-most TSS is NOT the lowest PWD, 
# and the value represents how much higher the divergence is compared to the true minimum.
gene_comparison <- dt_plot[, .(
  pwd_rank1 = mean_pwd_3pairs[rank_from_5p == 1],
  pwd_min = min(mean_pwd_3pairs),
  min_rank = rank_from_5p[which.min(mean_pwd_3pairs)]
), by = gene][!is.na(pwd_rank1)]

gene_comparison[, delta_pwd := pwd_rank1 - pwd_min]

# Sensitivity Plot: Distribution of Delta PWD for genes where 5' is NOT the lowest
p_sensitivity <- ggplot(gene_comparison[delta_pwd > 0], aes(x = delta_pwd)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 50, fill = "firebrick", alpha = 0.7) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 12) +
  labs(
    title = "Sensitivity of 5′-most TSS choice (Delta PWD)",
    subtitle = "PWD(5′-most) - PWD(True Min) for genes where 5′ is not the lowest",
    x = "Delta PWD (Difference in divergence)",
    y = "Count of Genes"
  )
print(p_sensitivity)
```
```{r}
library(MASS)

# Perform Spearman Correlation (Robust to non-linear/outlier data)
cor_test <- cor.test(dt_plot$rank_from_5p, dt_plot$mean_pwd_3pairs, method = "spearman")

cat(sprintf("Spearman Correlation (Rho): %.4f\n", cor_test$estimate))
cat(sprintf("P-value: %.2e\n", cor_test$p.value))

# Robust Scatterplot
# Note: method="rlm" requires the MASS package
if (!requireNamespace("MASS", quietly = TRUE)) install.packages("MASS")

p_corr <- ggplot(dt_plot, aes(x = rank_from_5p, y = mean_pwd_3pairs)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.1, size = 0.5) +
  geom_smooth(method = "rlm", color = "darkgreen", se = TRUE) +
  geom_quantile(quantiles = 0.5, color = "blue", size = 1) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 12) +
  labs(
    title = "Robust trend of PWD vs TSS Rank",
    subtitle = paste0("Spearman Rho = ", round(cor_test$estimate, 3)),
    x = "TSS Rank from 5' (1 = 5' most)",
    y = "Mean PWD across 3 Normal pairs"
  )
print(p_corr)
```
